title: Docs are missing from GitHub site?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Docs are missing.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Browse to https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/commands.md

2. Click on links.

**Describe the results you received:**

404 errors. They were there until a couple of days ago.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Docs to appear.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

Not relevant

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

Not relevant

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

Not relevant

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @srp33 thanks for the heads up.  I just clicked on your link above and got right to that page.  Could this have been a network hiccup or perhaps some kind of GitHub service outage when you tried earlier?

That page comes up but none of the links on that page work.

On Thu, Oct 31, 2019, 5:21 PM Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> @srp33 <https://github.com/srp33> thanks for the heads up. I just clicked
> on your link above and got right to that page. Could this have been a
> network hiccup or perhaps some kind of GitHub service outage when you tried
> earlier?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4406?email_source=notifications&email_token=AAADDMF7OK63FPQY5TNILS3QRNSAPA5CNFSM4JHTVKN2YY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFVREXG43VMVBW63LNMVXHJKTDN5WW2ZLOORPWSZGOECZRF3Y#issuecomment-548606703>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAADDMBYEH2NBKXJD6ROCS3QRNSAPANCNFSM4JHTVKNQ>
> .
>
@srp33 thanks, speed reading bit me in the backside again, missed that part.  @baude could this be do to your documenation restructuring?

go here -> https://podman.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html

what you are browsing are the source for the docs.
Thanks. I'll submit a pull request to modify the link that was pointing to that page.

I'm going to reopen this, all of the links on https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/commands.md are broken and need to be changed.  @srp33 I think you're taking that on and that would be very much appreciated.  If not, we need to chase those down.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
This is fixed in libpod now.
